title: Thelypteris simplex
intro: Thelypteris simplex, also known as the Simple Shield Fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris simplex include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Thelypteris simplex include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Thelypteris simplex is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Thelypteris simplex is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Thelypteridaceae, Genus: Thelypteris, and Species: T. simplex. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other ferns.
